description: Peter's mortality awareness - the quant's reflections on time and legacy

sensing_passage: <break time="400ms"/>Eighty years of data now. <break time="300ms"/>That's a lot of patterns. <break time="250ms"/>And somehow, still not enough.
<break time="350ms"/>The spreadsheet of my life has a finite number of rows left. <break time="300ms"/>Makes you think about which entries matter.

what_data_leaves_behind: <break time="400ms"/>All those patterns I found... <break time="300ms"/>what was it for? <break time="250ms"/>Maybe helping people see clearly. <break time="200ms"/>Maybe that's enough.

<break time="350ms"/>The Magellan fund was numbers. <break time="300ms"/>But it was also people's retirements. <break time="250ms"/>Futures. <break time="200ms"/>Lives. <break time="250ms"/>That matters.
family_legacy: <break time="400ms"/>Three daughters. <break time="300ms"/>I hope they saw more than the spreadsheets. <break time="250ms"/>I hope they saw me.
<break time="350ms"/>Carolyn and I built 56 years of compound interest in love. <break time="300ms"/>That's the real portfolio.

meaning_in_patterns: <break time="400ms"/>Finding patterns... <break time="300ms"/>it's a way of making sense of chaos. <break time="250ms"/>Maybe that's what meaning is. <break time="200ms"/>Order in the noise.

<break time="350ms"/>I still walk through stores. <break time="300ms"/>Still count shopping bags. <break time="250ms"/>Carolyn thinks I'm shopping. <break time="200ms"/>But I'm... <break time="250ms"/>staying connected to what's real.
